Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Answer
Off Calendar Jun-08-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - MARY OCHOA, TEL. NO. 1-707-480-5437, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. TRACY THOMPSON OF DAVIS WRIGHT TREMAINE LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-276-6564 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - HEARING HELD IN CAMERA. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT COVINGTON & BURLING LLP'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Answer is DENIED. Granting of the motion is not necessary for a fair resolution of this case and doing so would be unfairly prejudicial to plaintiff Mary Ochoa. Where, as here, an employee has worked for an employer for a lengthy period of time and the employer was not aware of any facts or circumstances supporting the proposed new affirmative defense, the assertion of such an affirmative defense based solely on the employee's mental health records and/or a mental health professional's review of those records and possible litigation-compelled mental health examination is not necessary to the fair adjudication of the case and places the employee in the extremely prejudicial position of having to explain her private thoughts and actions that have not in any way been connected to the employer. To allow such an affirmative defense would very possibly lead to the jury's deciding this case not based on the pertinent facts and circumstances, but on matters of little or no import to the parties' claims and defenses. Thus, while it is unusual to deny leave to amend based on the nature of the affirmative defense sought to be added, this motion presents compelling circumstances requiring that leave to amend be denied due to the nature of the affirmative defense sought to be added and the reasons proffered for doing so. The transcript can be transcribed only for appellate purpose. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 =302/hk

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-06-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - MARY OCHOA, TEL. NO. 1-707-480-5437, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. TRACY THOMPSON OF DAVIS WRIGHT LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-276-6564 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants Covington & Burling LLP, Timothy Hester, John Waters, Jeffrey Huvelle, Lindsay Burke, Annette Wigton, Nisa Walls, and Simon Frankel's demurrer to the sixth cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress and seventh cause of action for violation of privacy in the first amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to both causes of action. As to the sixth cause of action, plaintiff Mary Ochoa has not alleged facts showing that defendants engaged in extreme and outrageous conduct toward her. As to the seventh cause of action, Ms. Ochoa failed to allege an invasion of privacy sufficiently serious to constitute an egregious breach. (Hill v. Nat'l Collegiate Athletic Assn. (1994) 7 Cal.4th 1, 37.) Because it does not appear reasonably possible for Ms. Ochoa to allege sufficient facts to cure the deficiencies in her intentional infliction and privacy causes of action, Ms. Ochoa is not granted leave to amend. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk
